title: ulmus procera var. berandii
intro: Ulmus procera var. berandii, also known as the British elm or wych elm, is a deciduous tree species native to the UK and Europe. The medicinal properties of Ulmus procera var. berandii are attributed to its bark, which contains a compound called ulmusin, that has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support cardiovascular health. The herbal preparations of Ulmus procera var. berandii include a tea made from its bark, which can be used to treat various ailments, and a tincture that can be taken internally or applied topically.
